title: Meibomia prehensilis
taxonomy: Meibomia prehensilis is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the butterfly pea or butterfly milkweed. It is native to the southeastern United States. The plant is also known as Antennaria dioica and Clitoria mariana. It is classified as a perennial herb, with blue to purple flowers. Meibomia prehensilis is often found growing in moist areas and is a food source for various pollinators.
uses: Meibomia prehensilis, also known as the trumpet creeper or trumpet vine, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its roots and stems are said to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help soothe skin irritations and wounds. In some cultures, the plant is believed to have a calming effect on the nervous system, making it a potential treatment for anxiety and stress. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness for these purposes. The plant's medicinal uses are largely based on traditional knowledge and have not been extensively scientifically studied.

side_effects: Meibomia prehensilis, also known as Trumpet Creeper, is a plant used in traditional medicine, particularly by Native American communities.  It has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. However, the plant contains toxic compounds called cardenolides, which can cause side effects if ingested in large amounts. These side effects may include nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and in severe cases, cardiac problems. More research is needed to fully understand the potential risks associated with using Meibomia prehensilis as a medicinal plant.
intro: Meibomia prehensilis, also known as Trumpet Creeper, is a climbing plant native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its vibrant trumpet-shaped flowers and distinctive woody stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Meibomia prehensilis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Meibomia prehensilis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
